data.title: Costco's Kirkland energy drink is a disruptor despite limited supply
data.description:
Costco's (COST) new Kirkland Signature Sparkling Energy Drink is creating a stir in the industry, despite its limited availability. The zero‑sugar, lightly carbonated 12 oz energy drink could be positioned as a value alternative to brands like Celsius (CELH) if it is rolled out broadly. It comes in a 24‑can variety pack featuring peach, orange, and tropical flavors, with each can containing about 200 mg of caffeine, roughly 10 calories, added vitamins, and no sugar.Pricing has been one of the biggest talking points since each case is typically around $16.99, which substantially undercuts name‑brand competitors whose multipacks at Costco (COST) often land at roughly double that price on a per‑can basis.Notably, early reception has been broadly positive among Costco (COST) shoppers and energy drink fans, especially on taste and value. Online Costco (COST) communities and energy drink forums have been recommending it heavily to existing Celsius and Alani drinkers who want a cheaper daily option. The launch drew enough attention that it briefly rattled investors in Celsius Holdings (CELH).Evercore ISI reported that at a big recent regional meeting for Costco (COST) managers in the Northeast, all the stores were "clamoring to get their hands on the product" as soon as possible, which could signal a larger rollout is likely.

Elsewhere in the energy drink category, an increase in Red Bull promotional intensitywas later confirmed by Evercore through checks at Target (TGT), Amazon (AMZN), and Stop & Shop locations.

The recent developments in the energy drink sector could put pricing pressure on Celsius Holdings (CELH) and Monster Beverage (MNST).

data.title: Travel+Leisure Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.14, revenue of $961M beats by $6.17M
data.description:
• Travel+Leisure press release (TNL): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $961M (+2.9% Y/Y) beats by $6.17M.
  • Net revenue of $961 million. Gross VOI sales of $549 million, up 7% year-over-year
    • Volume per guest (VPG) of $3,321, a 3% increase year-over-year, on a 5% increase in tours
    • Expects second quarter Adjusted EBITDA of $260 million to $270 million and reaffirms full-year Adjusted EBITDA guidance of $1,030 million to $1,055 million

  • The Company is providing guidance for the second quarter 2026:

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $260 million to $270 million
    • Gross VOI sales of $660 million to $690 million
    • VPG of $3,200 to $3,250

  • The Company is reaffirming guidance for the 2026 full year:

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $1,030 million to $1,055 million
    • Gross VOI sales of $2.5 billion to $2.6 billion
    • VPG of $3,175 to $3,275

data.title: Westinghouse Air Brake reports mixed Q1 results; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Westinghouse Air Brake press release (WAB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.71 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $2.95B (+13.0% Y/Y) misses by $20M.
  • During the first quarter, the Company generated cash from operations of $199 million versus $191 million in the year ago period.
  • Adjusted Operating Margin Up 0.2 pts to 21.9%.
  • Strong Multi-year Backlog at $30.80 Billion; 12-month Backlog Growth at 12.8%

  • 2026 Financial Guidance

    • Wabtec raised its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance range to $10.25 - $10.65, raising it $0.20 at the midpoint, or up 16.5% (prior $10.05 to $10.45) vs. $10.45 consensus.
    • Wabtec continues to expect revenues to be between $12.19 billion to 12.49 billion, up 10.5% at the midpoint, (prior $12.19 billion to $12.49 billion) vs. $12.35B consensus.

data.title: GE Vernova beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• GE Vernova press release (GEV): Q1 GAAP EPS of $17.44 beats by $15.44.
  • Revenue of $9.34B (+16.3% Y/Y) beats by $90M.
  • 2026 Guidance GE Vernova is raising its 2026 financial guidance and now expects revenue of $44.5-$45.5 billion, up from $44-45 billion vs. $44.61B consensus, adjusted EBITDA margin* of 12%-14%, up from 11%-13%, and free cash flow* of $6.5-$7.5 billion, up from $5.0-$5.5 billion. Segment guidance is: • Power: 16%-18% organic revenue* growth and 17%-19% segment EBITDA margin, up from 16%-18%. • Electrification: Revenue of $14.0-$14.5 billion, up from $13.5-$14.0 billion, which includes approximately $3 billion from Prolec GE, and 18%-20% segment EBITDA margin, up from 17%-19%. • Wind: Organic revenue* down low-double digits and approximately $400 million of segment EBITDA losses.

data.title: Boston Scientific Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.01, revenue of $5.2B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Boston Scientific press release (BSX): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $5.2B (+11.6% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Reported net sales of $5.203 billion, representing an increase of 11.6 percent on a reported basis, compared to the company's guidance range of 10.5 to 12.0 percent; and 9.4 percent on an operational and organic basis, compared to the company's guidance range of 8.5 to 10.0 percent, all compared to the prior year period.
  • MedSurg: 7.8 percent reported, 5.7 percent operational and organic
  • Cardiovascular: 13.5 percent reported, 11.2 percent operational and organic
  • Achieved the following net sales growth in each region, compared to the prior year period:
    • United States (U.S.): 10.9 percent reported and operational
    • Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA): 10.1 percent reported and 1.2 percent operational
    • Asia-Pacific (APAC): 14.7 percent reported and 12.0 percent operational
    • Latin America and Canada (LACA): 19.0 percent reported and 12.0 percent operational
  • The company now estimates net sales growth for the full year 2026, versus the prior year period, to be approximately 7.0 to 8.5 percent on a reported basis and 6.5 to 8.0 percent on an organic basis. Full year organic net sales guidance excludes the impact of foreign currency fluctuations and net sales attributable to certain acquisitions and divestitures for which there are less than a full period of comparable net sales. The company now estimates adjusted EPS, excluding certain charges (credits), of $3.34 to $3.41 vs. $3.45 consensus
  • The company estimates net sales growth for the second quarter of 2026, versus the prior year period, to be approximately 5.5 to 7.5 percent on a reported basis and 5.0 to 7.0 percent on an organic basis. Second quarter organic net sales guidance excludes the impact of foreign currency fluctuations and net sales attributable to certain acquisitions and divestitures for which there are less than a full period of comparable net sales. The company estimates adjusted EPS, excluding certain charges (credits), of $0.82 to $0.84 vs. $0.86 consensus
  • Shares -2.7% PM.

data.title: Cango names new CFO amid Bitcoin mining pivot
data.description:
• Cango (CANG) appointed Simon Ming Yeung Tang as CFO, effective April 22, 2026.
  • Mr. Tang, previously the company's Chief Investment Officer, steps into the role amid its operations as a global Bitcoin miner.
  • Simon Ming Yeung Tang replaces Yongyi Zhang as CFO and also joins the board as a director.
  • This follows Cango's 2025 pivot to Bitcoin mining, which involved prior leadership shifts like Zhang's initial return as CFO in July 2025.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Otis Worldwide tops Q1 revenue estimates but misses on adjusted EPS; lifts FY26 revenue guidance
Cboe to offload Australia and Canada units to TMX Group for $300M
Prediction markets signal JD Vance unlikely to visit Pakistan this week
data.description:
Prediction markets are assigning low near-term odds to a potential Pakistan visit by U.S. Vice President JD Vance, with pricing on Kalshi implying the trip is unlikely before late April.

Kalshi contracts show roughly a 3% probability of a visit before April 23, rising to about 10% by April 24 and 22% by April 25. The curve steepens thereafter, reaching 33% before April 27 and 44% before April 30, suggesting traders see a narrow window at the end of the month where a trip becomes more plausible.

By early May, odds approach a coin toss. Pricing for a visit before May 4 stands near 50%, indicating growing but still uncertain expectations that diplomatic travel could materialize in the coming weeks.

On Polymarket, a separate set of contracts shows even lower near-term conviction. Markets priced a roughly 2% chance of a visit on April 22, rising to 6% for April 23 and about 12% for April 24, with sharp day-on-day declines reflecting fading expectations of an imminent trip.

Vance was expected to land in Pakistan on Wednesday. However, he called off the trip until further notice, as Tehran representatives refused to attend due to unreasonable U.S. demands.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Taylor Morrison Home Corporation GAAP EPS of $1.01 beats by $0.15, revenue of $1.39B beats by $60M
data.description:
• Taylor Morrison Home Corporation press release (TMHC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.01 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.39B (-26.8% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Home closings revenue of $1.3 billion
    • 2,268 closings at an average sales price of $578,000
  • Net sales orders of 2,914 at an average selling price of $603,000
    • Monthly net sales pace of 2.7 per community
    • Sales order backlog of 3,465 homes with a sales value of $2.3 billion
  • 75,626 homebuilding lots owned and controlled; 51% controlled off balance sheet
